info.name: ScoutRipper API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the ScoutRipper backend.

**Base URL:** `{{baseUrl}}` — set this variable to `http://localhost:8000` for local development.

**Authentication:** Protected routes require a session cookie set by `POST /api/auth/sign-in/email`. Postman automatically stores and forwards cookies.

**Vendor routes** additionally require `vendor_id` in the request body or query string — this is the numeric ID of the vendor record the authenticated user is operating on.

variable.description: Base URL of the ScoutRipper API server

request.description:
Returns API server status. Useful for uptime checks and verifying the server is reachable.

**Auth:** None

item.name: Auth (Better-Auth)
item.description: Authentication routes managed by Better-Auth. These endpoints handle session creation, destruction, and OAuth. The library parses its own request body — do NOT send `Content-Type: application/json` for sign-in/sign-up; instead use JSON body directly (Better-Auth accepts it).

request.description:
Register a new user with email and password.

**Auth:** None

**Body:**
- `email` (string, required) — unique email address
- `password` (string, required) — plain-text password, hashed by Better-Auth
- `name` (string, required) — display name

Authenticate with email and password. On success, Better-Auth sets a session cookie automatically. Postman stores this cookie and forwards it on subsequent requests to protected routes.

**Auth:** None

**Body:**
- `email` (string, required)
- `password` (string, required)

Invalidates the current session. Clears the session cookie.

**Auth:** Active session cookie required

Sends a password reset email via nodemailer (configured with `EMAIL_FROM` + `EMAIL_PASSWORD` env vars).

**Auth:** None

**Body:**
- `email` (string, required) — the registered email address

Consumes the reset token from the email and sets a new password.

**Auth:** None

**Body:**
- `token` (string, required) — the reset token from the email link
- `password` (string, required) — the new plain-text password

OAuth callback endpoint for Google. Better-Auth handles the token exchange and session creation internally. Initiate the flow by navigating to the Better-Auth Google sign-in URL.

**Auth:** None (handled by OAuth flow)

item.name: Treks
item.description: Public trek discovery endpoints. All routes are unauthenticated except `/me/wishlist`.

query.description: Location slug — uses ltree ancestry query
Sort order: price_asc | price_desc | duration_asc | newest (default)

request.description:
Paginated list of treks with filtering and sorting. Built with raw SQL for performance.

**Auth:** None

**Query Params:**
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `page` | number | Page number (default 1) |
| `limit` | number | Results per page (default 10) |
| `status` | string | Trek publish status (default 'publish') |
| `isFeatured` | string | Pass 'yes' for featured only |
| `categoryId` | number | Filter by destination category ID |
| `minPrice` / `maxPrice` | number | Price range |
| `duration` | number | Duration in days |
| `difficulty` | string | Difficulty attribute slug |
| `location` | string | Location slug — traverses ltree hierarchy |
| `category` | string | Attribute category slug |
| `season` | string | Season attribute slug |
| `sort` | string | `price_asc`, `price_desc`, `duration_asc`, `newest` |

Returns treks with `isFeatured = 'yes'`, ordered by newest. Used for homepage/landing carousels.

**Auth:** None

Returns all treks within a location and all its sub-locations using ltree `<@` ancestry traversal.

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `slug` — location slug (e.g. `uttarakhand`)

Placeholder endpoint — not yet implemented. Returns a greeting with the user's name.

**Auth:** Required (session cookie)

> Use `GET /api/v1/wishlist` for the real wishlist functionality.

Full trek object including location, category, attributes, expedition team, computed min_price, listing count, and leader count.

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `slug` — the trek's unique slug

Returns the location ancestor hierarchy for a trek (e.g. India → Uttarakhand → Uttarkashi). Uses ltree `@>` ancestor query.

**Auth:** None

Returns all route variants for a trek, each with day-by-day itinerary and processed image URLs.

**Auth:** None

Returns up to 6 sibling locations at the same ltree depth as the trek's location, scored by name/token similarity.

**Auth:** None

Treks in the same location subtree, each enriched with up to 3 trek leader names/images (batched, no N+1).

**Auth:** None

All active listings for a trek. Each listing includes vendor, optional trek leader, and the next available `open` slot (via SQL LATERAL join).

**Auth:** None

item.name: Listings
item.description: Public endpoints for reading listing details and reviews.

request.description:
Full listing detail including vendor, trek leader, parent trek summary, and all upcoming `open` / `sold_out` slots.

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `id` — numeric listing ID

Paginated reviews for a listing with aggregate rating breakdown (guide expertise, safety, logistics, sustainability).

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `id` — numeric listing ID

item.name: Bookings
item.description: All booking routes require an active session (cookie-based auth). Scope is always the authenticated user — users can only see and create their own bookings.

request.description:
Creates a booking and atomically decrements available seats inside a transaction. Uses an optimistic lock (re-reads seat count inside the transaction) to prevent double-booking under concurrent requests.

**Auth:** Required

**Body:**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `listing_id` | number | ✅ | ID of the trek listing |
| `slot_id` | number | ✅ | ID of the trek slot |
| `adult_count` | number | ✅ | Must be ≥ 1 |
| `children_count` | number | — | Defaults to 0 if omitted |
| `special_request` | string | — | Free-text request |

**Error Responses:**
- `400` — missing required fields or not enough seats
- `404` — listing/slot not found or listing not active
- `500 SEAT_CONFLICT` — concurrent booking race (retry)

Returns all bookings for the authenticated user, ordered by newest first. Each booking includes inline listing + vendor + slot summary.

**Auth:** Required

Returns a single booking scoped to the authenticated user. Includes the parent trek object (id, title, slug, banner_image) in addition to the listing and slot.

**Auth:** Required

**Path Param:** `id` — numeric booking ID

item.name: Wishlist
item.description: User wishlist for treks and experiences. All routes require authentication and are scoped to the current user.

request.description:
Returns all wishlist items for the authenticated user. Each item is enriched with the full trek or experience details fetched in a single batch query.

**Auth:** Required

Adds a trek or experience to the user's wishlist.

**Auth:** Required

**Body:**
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `item_type` | string | `'trek'` or `'experience'` |
| `item_id` | number | Numeric ID of the trek/experience |
| `item_slug` | string | Slug of the trek/experience |

**Error Responses:**
- `400` — item already in wishlist

Removes a wishlist item by its wishlist record ID.

**Auth:** Required

**Path Param:** `id` — numeric wishlist item ID

**Error Responses:**
- `400` — invalid ID
- `403` — item belongs to another user
- `404` — item not found

item.name: Locations
item.description: Location hierarchy using PostgreSQL ltree extension.

request.description:
Returns top-level locations (ltree depth ≤ 2) with trek counts. Trek count includes all published treks in the entire location subtree.

**Auth:** None

Full location detail including breadcrumb ancestors, direct children with trek counts, gallery images, general info, and map coordinates.

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `slug` — location slug (e.g. `uttarakhand`, `himachal-pradesh`)

Returns all destination categories with trek counts.

**Auth:** None

Paginated list of published treks in a given destination category.

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `slug` — category slug

item.name: Experiences
item.description: Public endpoints for experiences (activities, day trips, etc.).

request.description:
Paginated list of experiences.

**Auth:** None

**Query Params:** `page`, `limit`, `status` (default 'publish'), `isFeatured`

Top 6 featured experiences (`is_featured = 'yes'`).

**Auth:** None

Experiences within a location and its entire subtree using ltree `<@` query.

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `slug` — location slug

Full experience detail with banner/feature/gallery images (all processed through `buildImageUrl`), location, and attributes grouped by attribute category.

**Auth:** None

**Path Param:** `slug` — experience slug

Returns the authenticated user's profile: `id`, `name`, `email`, `image` (CDN URL), `emailVerified`, `role`, `createdAt`.

**Auth:** Required

Partial update of the authenticated user's profile. Only fields provided are updated.

**Auth:** Required

**Body (all optional):**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `name` | string | Display name |
| `image` | string | Storage key or URL. Empty string sets image to null |

item.name: Reviews
item.description: Review submission and helpfulness voting. Reviews require a verified booking.

body.raw:
{
  "vendor_id": 1,
  "listing_id": 1,
  "trek_id": 3,
  "rating": 5,
  "trek_leader_id": 2,
  "title": "Amazing trek experience!",
  "body": "The guides were professional and the route was breathtaking.",
  "guide_expertise": 5,
  "safety_protocols": 4,
  "logistics_gear": 5,
  "sustainability": 4
}

request.description:
Submits a review. The user must have a completed booking for the listing. Duplicate reviews are rejected.

**Auth:** Required

**Body:**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `vendor_id` | number | ✅ | Vendor ID |
| `listing_id` | number | ✅ | Listing ID |
| `trek_id` | number | ✅ | Trek ID |
| `rating` | number | ✅ | Overall rating (1–5) |
| `trek_leader_id` | number | — | Optional leader reference |
| `title` | string | — | Review headline |
| `body` | string | — | Review text |
| `guide_expertise` | number | — | Sub-rating 1–5 |
| `safety_protocols` | number | — | Sub-rating 1–5 |
| `logistics_gear` | number | — | Sub-rating 1–5 |
| `sustainability` | number | — | Sub-rating 1–5 |

**Error Responses:**
- `400` — missing required fields or duplicate review
- `403` — user has no verified booking for this listing
- `404` — listing not found

Vote on a review's helpfulness.

**Auth:** Required

**Path Param:** `id` — numeric review ID

**Body:**
- `type` (string, required) — vote type, e.g. `'helpful'` or `'not_helpful'`

item.name: Vendor Dashboard
item.description:
All vendor routes require an active session. `vendor_id` is passed in the request body (POST/PATCH) or as a query parameter (GET/DELETE) to identify which vendor the user is acting on behalf of.

> **Business rules enforced:**
> - Slots cannot be created more than 365 days in the future
> - `end_date` must be ≥ `start_date`
> - A vendor may not have more than **1000 open slots** across all their listings
> - A vendor may not create more than **100 slots per hour** (rate-limited, HTTP 429)
> - Vendors can only set slot status to `vendor_cancel_requested` — setting `cancelled` directly returns 403
> - Valid slot statuses: `open`, `sold_out`, `completed`, `cancelled`, `vendor_cancel_requested`

request.description:
Returns all published treks for use in the vendor listing creation form (trek picker dropdown). Includes id, title, slug, bannerImage, and location name.

**Auth:** Required

body.raw:
{
  "vendor_id": 1,
  "trek_id": 3,
  "title": "Kedarkantha 6-Day Premium Package",
  "difficulty": "moderate",
  "duration_days": 6,
  "price": 12000,
  "sale_price": 10500,
  "route_name": "Classic Route via Juda Ka Talab",
  "meeting_point": "Sankri Village Base Camp",
  "distance_km": "48",
  "elevation_gain": "3100m",
  "ascent_time": "4-5 hours",
  "descent_time": "3-4 hours",
  "intro_text": "Experience the magic of Kedarkantha with our premium guided package.",
  "cancellation_policy": "Full refund if cancelled 15 days before start date.",
  "itinerary": [{"day": 1, "title": "Drive to Sankri", "description": "..."}],
  "inclusions": ["Accommodation", "All meals", "Expert guide"],
  "exclusions": ["Travel to Sankri", "Personal expenses"],
  "highlights": ["Summit at 12,500 ft", "Snow-covered trail"],
  "tags": ["winter", "snow", "moderate"]
}

request.description:
Creates a new listing in `draft` status. A listing becomes `active` automatically when the first batch of slots is added via `POST /vendor/listings/:id/slots`.

**Auth:** Required

**Required fields:** `vendor_id`, `trek_id`

**Response:** `{ success: true, data: { id: "<listingId>" } }`

Partial update of a listing. Only provided fields are changed. The vendor must own the listing.

**Auth:** Required

**Required in body:** `vendor_id`

**Error Responses:**
- `403` — listing belongs to a different vendor
- `404` — listing not found

Returns all listings belonging to the vendor with their slot counts and trek summary.

**Auth:** Required

**Query Param:** `vendor_id` (required)

Full listing detail for vendor dashboard — includes ALL slots (no date/status filter, so past and draft slots are visible too), vendor info, trek leader, and trek images.

**Auth:** Required

**Error Responses:**
- `403` — listing belongs to a different vendor
- `404` — not found

Creates one or more slots for a listing. If the listing is in `draft` status it is automatically promoted to `active`.

**Auth:** Required

**Business Rules:**
- `end_date` must be ≥ `start_date` — returns `400` otherwise
- `start_date` must be within 365 days of today — returns `400` otherwise
- Vendor must not already have ≥ 1000 `open` slots across all listings — returns `400`
- Vendor must not have created ≥ 100 slots in the past hour — returns `429`

**Error Responses:**
- `400` — validation failure or open-slot cap exceeded
- `403` — listing belongs to another vendor
- `404` — listing not found
- `429` — rate limit exceeded (100 slots/hour)

Updates a slot's `total_seats` and/or `status`. When `total_seats` changes, `available_seats` is recomputed automatically.

**Auth:** Required

**Status Enum:** `open` · `sold_out` · `completed` · `cancelled` · `vendor_cancel_requested`

**Business Rules:**
- Vendors **cannot** set status to `cancelled` directly — use `vendor_cancel_requested` instead (returns `403`)
- Unknown status values return `400`

**Error Responses:**
- `400` — invalid status value
- `403` — slot belongs to another vendor, or attempted direct cancellation
- `404` — slot not found

query.description: Numeric vendor ID (passed as query param since DELETE has no standard body)

request.description:
Permanently deletes a slot. Only allowed if the slot has zero bookings (`booked_seats = 0`).

**Auth:** Required

**Error Responses:**
- `400` — slot has existing bookings and cannot be deleted
- `403` — slot belongs to another vendor
- `404` — slot not found
